summary: Filter auth login cookie jar to extract only the auth_token JWT, dropping cookies whose values contain Chrome's raw encrypted_value bytes.
reason: On macOS, pycookiecheat sometimes returns Chrome's encrypted_value blob unchanged for cookies like _fbp, client_id, _fbp. Even the auth_token cookie can have a binary prefix before the JWT. Storing those bytes in TOML breaks config.Load on the next run with `toml: invalid character U+00D9`. The fix scans for the JWT pattern (eyJ...eyJ...) inside the auth_token cookie value and drops every other cookie — Skool's API only authenticates with auth_token.
validated_outcome: Fresh `auth login --chrome` on macOS Studio writes ASCII TOML; `posts top` returns real ranked results.
upstream_issue: cli-printing-press: cookie extraction backend could surface a per-CLI hook for jar sanitization; today, every printed CLI that uses cookie auth has to hand-roll this filter.
